segments.text: About that time, Judah left his brothers and settled near a man named Hirah, an Adullamite.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: There Judah saw the daughter of a Canaanite man named Shua, and he took her as a wife and slept with her.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: So she conceived and gave birth to a son, and Judah named him Er.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Again she conceived and gave birth to a son, and she named him Onan.
Then she gave birth to another son and named him Shelah; it was at Chezib that she gave birth to him.
Now Judah acquired a wife for Er, his firstborn, and her name was Tamar.
But Er, Judah’s firstborn, was wicked in the sight of the LORD; so the LORD put him to death.
Then Judah said to Onan,
Sleep with your brother’s wife. Perform your duty as her brother-in-law and raise up offspring for your brother.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: But Onan knew that the offspring would not belong to him; so whenever he would sleep with his brother’s wife, he would spill his seed on the ground so that he would not produce offspring for his brother.
What he did was wicked in the sight of the LORD, so He put Onan to death as well.
Then Judah said to his daughter-in-law Tamar,
Live as a widow in your father’s house until my son Shelah grows up.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: For he thought,
He may die too, like his brothers.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: So Tamar went to live in her father’s house.
After a long time Judah’s wife, the daughter of Shua, died. When Judah had finished mourning, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite went up to his sheepshearers at Timnah.
When Tamar was told,
Your father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep,
segments.speaker: Tamar was

segments.text: she removed her widow’s garments, covered her face with a veil to disguise herself, and sat at the entrance to Enaim, which is on the way to Timnah. For she saw that although Shelah had grown up, she had not been given to him as a wife.
When Judah saw her, he thought she was a prostitute because she had covered her face.
Not realizing that she was his daughter-in-law, he went over to her and said,
Come now, let me sleep with you.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: What will you give me for sleeping with you?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: she inquired.
I will send you a young goat from my flock,
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text:
Judah answered.
But she replied,
Only if you leave me something as a pledge until you send it.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: What pledge should I give you?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
he asked.
She answered,
Your seal and your cord, and the staff in your hand.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So he gave them to her and slept with her, and she became pregnant by him.
Then Tamar got up and departed. And she removed her veil and put on her widow’s garments again.
Now when Judah sent his friend Hirah the Adullamite with the young goat to collect the items he had left with the woman, he could not find her.
He asked the men of that place,
Where is the shrine prostitute who was beside the road at Enaim?
segments.speaker: Hirah

segments.text: No shrine prostitute has been here,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they answered.
So Hirah returned to Judah and said,
I could not find her, and furthermore, the men of that place said, ‘No shrine prostitute has been here.’
segments.speaker: Hirah

segments.text: Let her keep the items,
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: Judah replied.
Otherwise we will become a laughingstock. After all, I did send her this young goat, but you could not find her.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: About three months later, Judah was told,
Your daughter-in-law Tamar has prostituted herself, and now she is pregnant.
segments.speaker: About three months later, Judah was

segments.text: Bring her out!
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: Judah replied.
Let her be burned to death!
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: As she was being brought out, Tamar sent a message to her father-in-law:
I am pregnant by the man to whom these items belong.
segments.speaker: Tamar

segments.text: And she added,
Please examine them. Whose seal and cord and staff are these?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Judah recognized the items and said,
She is more righteous than I, since I did not give her to my son Shelah.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: And he did not have relations with her again.

text: The Birth of Perez and Zerah

segments.text: When the time came for Tamar to give birth, there were twins in her womb.
And as she was giving birth, one of them put out his hand; so the midwife took a scarlet thread and tied it around his wrist.
This one came out first,
segments.speaker: Midwife

segments.text: she announced.
But when he pulled his hand back and his brother came out, she said,
You have broken out first!
segments.speaker: Midwife

segments.text: So he was named Perez.
Then his brother came out with the scarlet thread around his wrist, and he was named Zerah.
